key_insight: MKGR improves cold-start protein-protein interaction prediction by combining protein sequence features with multiple biomedical knowledge graphs so the model can still reason about proteins with no training-network edges.
clinical_relevance: Better PPI prediction can support target biology, disease-mechanism research, and downstream drug discovery when interaction data are sparse or incomplete.
method: Region-aware sequence encoder captures context from structurally informed protein sequence regions.
Graph attention encoders learn protein embeddings from four modality-specific knowledge graphs: protein-drug, protein-disease, protein-miRNA, and protein-lncRNA.
A bridge reconstruction loss and pair-level gating module fuse sequence and graph evidence for each protein pair.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports evaluation on two benchmark datasets in novel-old and novel-novel cold-start settings, with MKGR outperforming sequence, network, and knowledge-graph baselines on ACC, F1, AUC, AUPR, and MCC.
watch_item: Performance is benchmark-based, so real-world utility may depend on how well the multimodal graphs and sequence annotations generalize to new proteins and datasets.
paper_title: MKGR: Multimodal Knowledge-Graph Representation Learning for Cold-Start Protein-Protein Interaction Prediction
